text: The plan below assumes ~2h/day for 13 weeks (~91 days). Adapt to your time: if you have 1h/day, double the duration. Abbreviations: L = LeetCode, D = difficulty, P = day's problems. Philosophy Patterns > volume . 5 problems of the same category > 5 random problems. Time-boxing . Easy 15 min, Medium 25 min, Hard 40 min. If exceeded, read the solution. One weekly revision . Every Sunday, redo 5 old problems. Mocks from day 50 onwards . Start early, don't wait to be "ready". Initial setup (days 1-3) Open LeetCode account (premium recommended after first 30 days if serious). Install an editor with Python. Create a Google Sheet "FAANG log" with: date, problem, difficulty, time spent, result (alone / hint / solution), pattern. Print ch. 22 — Coding Patterns . Week 1 — Fundamentals Goal : fluent Big-O, arrays/strings mastered. Day Ch. Problems 1 01 All ch. 01 exercises 2 02 Two Sum, Best Time Buy/Sell, Contains Duplicate 3 02 Product Except Self, Maximum Subarray 4 02 3Sum, Longest Substring Without Repeating 5 02 Valid Palindrome, Group Anagrams, Container With Most Water 6 02 Longest Palindromic Substring, Trapping Rain Water 7 recap Redo 5 problems you got wrong this week Week 2 — Hashmap, linked list, stack Day Ch. Problems 8 03 Subarray Sum Equals K, Longest Consecutive Sequence 9 03 Top K Frequent, Minimum Window Substring 10 04 Reverse Linked List, Merge Two Sorted, Cycle 11 04 Remove Nth from End, Cycle II, Palindrome LL 12 04 Add Two Numbers, Copy with Random, LRU Cache 13 05 Valid Parentheses, Min Stack, Daily Temperatures 14 recap Largest Rectangle in Histogram, Decode String Week 3 — Trees Day Ch. Problems 15 06 Max Depth, Invert, Same Tree, Symmetric 16 06 Balanced, Diameter, Path Sum II 17 06 Validate BST, LCA, Right Side View 18 06 Kth Smallest in BST, Level Order 19 06 Serialize/Deserialize, Max Path Sum 20 06 Construct from Pre+Inorder 21 recap 5 old problems of choice + 1 new Week 4 — Heap, two pointers, sliding window Day Ch. Problems 22 07 Kth Largest, K Closest Points 23 07 Merge K Sorted Lists, Median from Stream 24 07 Meeting Rooms II, Task Scheduler 25 12 Permutation in String, Longest Repeating Char Replacement 26 12 Fruit Into Baskets, Subarrays K Different 27 12 Squares of Sorted Array, Sliding Window Max 28 recap Week 5 — Base graphs Day Ch. Problems 29 08 Number of Islands, Clone Graph 30 08 Course Schedule I & II 31 08 Pacific Atlantic, Rotting Oranges (BFS multi) 32 08 Word Ladder, Word Search 33 08 Network Delay (Dijkstra), Cheapest Flights with K Stops 34 08 Redundant Connection (DSU), Min Cost Connect Points (MST) 35 recap Alien Dictionary, Word Search II (with trie) Week 6 — Trie, recursion, backtracking Day Ch. Problems 36 09 Implement Trie, Word Dictionary (wildcard) 37 09 Word Search II (redo with trie), Replace Words 38 10 Subsets, Permutations 39 10 Combination Sum, Combinations 40 10 Letter Combinations, Generate Parentheses 41 10 Word Search, Palindrome Partitioning 42 recap N-Queens or Sudoku Solver Week 7 — Sorting, binary search Day Ch. Problems 43 11 Search Insert, First Bad Version 44 11 Search in Rotated Sorted, Find Minimum in Rotated 45 11 Find Peak, First and Last Position 46 11 Koko Eating Bananas, Capacity to Ship 47 11 Sort Colors (Dutch flag), Wiggle Sort II 48 11 Quickselect (Kth Largest), Median of Two Sorted Arrays 49 recap Week 8 — Greedy + bit manipulation Day Ch. Problems 50 13 Jump Game I & II 51 13 Gas Station, Non-overlapping Intervals 52 13 Merge Intervals, Insert Interval 53 13 Partition Labels, Min Arrows 54 15 Single Number I & II, Counting Bits 55 15 Missing Number, Reverse Bits 56 Start mock interviews First mock on Pramp Week 9 — DP 1D Day Ch. Problems 57 14 Climbing Stairs, House Robber I & II 58 14 Coin Change I & II 59 14 LIS (both methods), Word Break 60 14 Partition Equal Subset Sum, Combination Sum IV 61 14 Max Product Subarray, Decode Ways 62 14 Stock with Cooldown 63 mock Second mock Week 10 — DP 2D + hard Day Ch. Problems 64 14 Unique Paths, Min Path Sum 65 14 Longest Common Subsequence 66 14 Edit Distance 67 14 Regular Expression Matching 68 14 Burst Balloons, Maximum Rectangle 69 DP recap 3 DP problems of varying difficulty 70 mock Third mock Week 11 — OOP, System design, concurrency Day Ch. What to do 71 16 Study + Design Parking Lot + Tic-Tac-Toe 72 16 Snake game + LRU as class + Elevator 73 17 Study fundamentals 74 18 Design TinyURL — write on paper, 35 min 75 18 Design Twitter — 45 min 76 18 Design Chat (WhatsApp) — 45 min 77 19 Study + Producer/Consumer, Print in Order, H2O Week 12 — DB, OS/networking, behavioral Day Ch. What to do 78 20 Study + 5 SQL exercises 79 20 Window functions: Department Top Three, Consecutive Numbers 80 21 Study. Practice the 15 quick questions out loud 81 23 Write your 10 STAR stories 82 23 Practice 10 stories out loud, record yourself 83 23 Refine 10 stories based on recording feedback 84 mock Behavioral mock (with friend or coach) Week 13 — Refinement + multiple mocks Day What 85 Coding mock + review 86 Redo 5 problems you got wrong in the first 4 weeks 87 System design mock 88 Redo 5 hard DP / graphs problems 89 Coding mock on hard problem 90 Complete loop mock (3-4 rounds) with friend 91 Rest. Tomorrow: apply! Tracking template Keep a sheet like this: Week # problems done Easy Medium Hard Mock Notes 1 14 8 6 0 0 ... 2 12 4 7 1 0 ... By the end of 13 weeks you should have ~150-180 problems solved. Checklist "ready for the loop" [ ] 150+ problems solved, of which ≥ 75 medium and ≥ 20 hard. [ ] You solve a random medium in ≤ 25 min 70% of the time. [ ] You can describe 3+ systems end-to-end out loud. [ ] You have 10 STAR stories written and rehearsed. [ ] You've done at least 5 total mock interviews. [ ] You can declare time + space complexity for every solution. [ ] You know the 16 coding patterns (chapter 22) by heart. When you can check all boxes: apply . Don't wait to feel "completely ready" — that moment never arrives. The loop itself will teach you new things. Good luck.
